LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUN-12-2020 HEARING HELD RE: INTERVENOR AIG PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE COMPLAINT-IN-INTERVENTION. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A COMPLAINT-IN-INTERVENTION IS GRANTED. THAT COMPLAINT SHALL BE FILED ON OR BEFORE JUNE 22, 2020. COURT TO SIGN INTERVENOR'S PROPOSED ORDER IF THE PROPOSED ORDER TRACKS THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JORDAN B. EVERAKES, Esq. of Grotefeld Hoffmann Schleiter Gordon & Ochoa, LLP (Tel: 415-344-9670) appearing via CourtCall for Intervenor AIG Property Casualty Company;
No appearances made by any other parties.

HEARING RE: INTERVENOR AIG PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY'S Motion For Leave To File Complaint-In-Intervention.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Travelers Property Casualty Company of America's unopposed motion for leave to file a complaint-in-intervention is granted. That complaint shall be filed on or before June 22, 2020.

Court to sign Intervenor's proposed order if the proposed order tracks the Court's tentative ruling.
